root_text.sn35.91:1.1: “Ejā, bhikkhave, rogo, ejā gaṇḍo, ejā sallaṁ.
root_text.sn35.91:1.2: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, tathāgato anejo viharati vītasallo.
root_text.sn35.91:1.3: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cepi ākaṅkheyya ‘anejo vihareyyaṁ vītasallo’ti,
root_text.sn35.91:1.4: cakkhuṁ na maññeyya, cakkhusmiṁ na maññeyya, cakkhuto na maññeyya, cakkhu meti na maññeyya;
root_text.sn35.91:1.8: yampidaṁ cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññeyya, tasmimpi na maññeyya, tatopi na maññeyya, taṁ meti na maññeyya.
root_text.sn35.91:1.9: Yañhi, bhikkhave, maññati, yasmiṁ maññati, yato maññati, yaṁ meti maññati, tato taṁ hoti aññathā.
root_text.sn35.91:2.1: Jivhaṁ na maññeyya, jivhāya na maññeyya, jivhāto na maññeyya, jivhā meti na maññeyya;
root_text.sn35.91:2.5: yampidaṁ jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññeyya, tasmimpi na maññeyya, tatopi na maññeyya, taṁ meti na maññeyya.
root_text.sn35.91:2.6: Yañhi, bhikkhave, maññati, yasmiṁ maññati, yato maññati, yaṁ meti maññati, tato taṁ hoti aññathā. Aññathābhāvī bhavasatto loko bhavameva abhinandati …pe….
root_text.sn35.91:3.1: Manaṁ na maññeyya, manasmiṁ na maññeyya, manato na maññeyya, mano meti na maññeyya …
root_text.sn35.91:3.4: yampidaṁ manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati vedayitaṁ sukhaṁ vā dukkhaṁ vā adukkhamasukhaṁ vā tampi na maññeyya, tasmimpi na maññeyya, tatopi na maññeyya, taṁ meti na maññeyya.
root_text.sn35.91:3.5: Yañhi, bhikkhave, maññati, yasmiṁ maññati, yato maññati, yaṁ meti maññati, tato taṁ hoti aññathā.
root_text.sn35.91:4.1: Yāvatā, bhikkhave, khandhadhātuāyatanā tampi na maññeyya, tasmimpi na maññeyya, tatopi na maññeyya, taṁ meti na maññeyya.
root_text.sn35.91:4.2: So evaṁ amaññamāno na kiñci loke upādiyati.
root_text.sn35.91:4.3: Anupādiyaṁ na paritassati. Aparitassaṁ paccattaññeva parinibbāyati.
root_text.sn35.91:4.4: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.91:1.1: “Mendicants, turbulence is a disease, a boil, a dart.
translation_text.sn35.91:1.2: That’s why the Realized One lives unperturbed, with dart drawn out.
translation_text.sn35.91:1.3: Now, a mendicant might wish: ‘May I live unperturbed, with dart drawn out.’
translation_text.sn35.91:1.4: So let them not conceive anything to be the eye, let them not conceive it in the eye, let them not conceive it as the eye, let them not conceive that ‘the eye is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.91:1.8: Let them not conceive anything to be the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on eye contact. Let them not conceive it in that, let them not conceive it as that, and let them not conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.91:1.9: For whatever you conceive it to be, whatever you conceive it in, whatever you conceive it as, and whatever you conceive to be ‘mine’: that becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.91:1.10: The world is attached to being, taking pleasure only in being, yet it becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.91:2.1: Let them not conceive anything to be the ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.91:3.1: Let them not conceive anything to be the mind …
translation_text.sn35.91:3.4: Let them not conceive anything to be the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact. Let them not conceive it in that, let them not conceive it as that, and let them not conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.91:3.5: For whatever you conceive it to be, whatever you conceive it in, whatever you conceive it as, and whatever you conceive to be ‘mine’: that becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.91:3.6: The world is attached to being, taking pleasure only in being, yet it becomes something else.
translation_text.sn35.91:4.1: As far as the aggregates, elements, and sense fields extend, they don’t conceive anything to be that, they don’t conceive it in that, they don’t conceive it as that, and they don’t conceive that ‘that is mine.’
translation_text.sn35.91:4.2: Not conceiving, they don’t grasp at anything in the world.
translation_text.sn35.91:4.3: Not grasping, they’re not anxious. Not being anxious, they personally become extinguished.
translation_text.sn35.91:4.4: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
